nodes.title: USDC Lenders
nodes.summary: USDC suppliers who back the shared senior dollar. They deposit through the zap and hold zipUSD; the redemption queue is their exit at par.

nodes.title: HELOC Originators
nodes.summary: Home-equity originators who open isolated credit lines against their liens, draw USDC, and repurchase by end of term.

nodes.title: Euler Vault Kit
nodes.summary: A system for constructing credit vaults: ERC-4626 vaults with borrowing added, deployed as proxies from a shared GenericFactory. Each vault is a passive lending pool with its own collateral list, oracle, interest model, and governor, so risk stays inside the vault that took it. Zipcode mints a fresh pair of EVK vaults for every credit line — a bare escrow vault holding the lien token and an isolated USDC borrow vault — and zipUSD itself is an EVK ESynth: Euler's mintable/burnable synthetic dollar token (not a vault), deployed with the constructor (EVC, "Zipcode USD", "zipUSD") and minted only by capacity-granted minters.

nodes.title: Ethereum Vault Connector
nodes.summary: The foundational layer that mediates between vaults: it tracks which vault controls an account's debt, batches calls, and lets an account authorize an operator to act on its behalf. Every address owns 256 sub-accounts sharing a 19-byte prefix, which is how one owner can hold many isolated positions. Zipcode leans on exactly this: each credit line gets a fresh LineAccount that registers its own EVC prefix and grants the venue adapter the operator bit over a code-free sub-account, and the gating hook asks the EVC one question — is the borrower's account operator-authorized to the borrow driver.

nodes.title: USDC Reserve Vault
nodes.summary:
The USDC Reserve vault holds idle supply, waiting to be allocated to a qualified credit line.

Chainlink CRE runs Allocation through Euler Earn.

docLink.label: Read more, in the "Why Euler?" Blog

nodes.title: Euler Price Oracles
nodes.summary: A library of modular oracle adapters behind one interface, IPriceOracle: ask for a quote (how much USDC is this much of that token worth) instead of a raw price. EVK vaults resolve collateral value through a router (EulerRouter) that maps each asset pair to whichever adapter the governor configured. Zipcode gives every credit line its own EulerRouter, wired once to price the line's lien token through the ZipcodeOracleRegistry (itself an IPriceOracle adapter) and then frozen — governance transferred to address(0) so nobody can ever re-point the line's price source.

nodes.title: EVK Hooks
nodes.summary: Every EVK vault carries a hook config: a target contract plus a bitfield of operations (borrow, deposit, liquidate, ...) that must consult it. When a hooked operation runs, the vault calls the target with the original calldata plus the EVC-authenticated caller appended; if the hook call fails, the operation fails. It is how a vault governor injects custom policy without touching vault code. Zipcode installs its CREGatingHook on every per-line borrow vault with exactly OP_BORROW | OP_LIQUIDATE hooked — and never OP_REPAY, so a line can always be repaid.

nodes.title: EulerSwap
nodes.summary: Euler's AMM built on top of its own lending infrastructure: pool capital sits inside Euler vaults, so the same assets earn lending yield, serve as collateral, and fill swaps, with just-in-time liquidity (borrowing the output token against the input) simulating far deeper books than a plain AMM. Euler's own synth documentation uses EulerSwap pools — not a peg-stability module — as how ESynth dollars hold their peg. Since zipUSD is an ESynth, this is the natural route for its secondary-market liquidity — but note this is the liquidity-bootstrap plan, not shipped code: the zipcode-euler repo contains no EulerSwap integration and its docs do not mention it.

nodes.title: ZipcodeController
nodes.summary: The orchestrator that opens, draws on, and closes credit lines when an off-chain Chainlink keeper sends it a typed instruction. Origination is one all-or-nothing batch — create the lien token, open the line, seed its price, set limits, fund, draw to the off-ramp, record, bump the silo count — and any failure rolls the whole thing back, including the freshly deployed contracts. It never touches a lending venue directly: every venue effect goes through the venue-neutral interface, and draws and closes re-resolve the venue from the line's stored silo so a retired silo can never strand a line.

nodes.title: EulerVenueAdapter
nodes.summary: The implementation of IZipcodeVenue — the venue-neutral interface (open, set limits, fund, draw, read debt, close; only plain values cross it) — against Euler, and the only venue built today. For each line it mints an isolated cluster: a fresh LineAccount, a bare escrow vault for the lien collateral, an isolated USDC borrow vault with the gating hook installed, and a per-line price router frozen at birth. It also charges the per-draw origination fee, installs the roughly 7.5% flat line interest rate, routes the curator's share of interest to the curator vault, and reclaims both EulerEarn queue slots on close so a pool can churn lines forever.

eulerPrimitive.how: It deploys two EVK vault proxies per line from the GenericFactory — a governance-stripped escrow vault for the lien and an isolated USDC borrow vault — and funds them from the EulerEarn pool it curates.

nodes.title: LineAccount
nodes.summary: A tiny constructor-only contract, CREATE2-deployed by the adapter for each line with the lien id as its salt. Its deterministic address establishes a fresh EVC owner-prefix, and in its constructor it grants the adapter the operator bit over its code-free borrow account — sub-account 1 of its own prefix, reached by XOR-ing the low bit. That one grant is what isolates every line's debt and collateral from every other line; after birth the contract is inert, and the cluster is abandoned when the line closes.

eulerPrimitive.how: Its constructor calls EVC.setAccountOperator on its own prefix-sharing, code-free sub-account, registering itself as prefix owner and authorizing the adapter as the borrow path's operator.

nodes.title: CREGatingHook
nodes.summary:
The CREGatingHook restricts access to borrows until the Bittensor zkProof Credit Oracle approves that borrower.

Repay stays permissionless.

eulerPrimitive.how: It is an EVK IHookTarget installed with OP_BORROW | OP_LIQUIDATE on each line's borrow vault, decoding the EVC-authenticated account the vault appends as trailing calldata.

docLink.label: Read more about Isolated Credit Lines

nodes.title: Lien token + factory
nodes.summary:
The LienCollateralToken is a 1/1 identity-and-collateral token, whose entire purpose is to fill the collateral slot required to enable a Line of Credit.

The value of a Lien Collateral Token is determined by its corresponding oracle.

eulerPrimitive.how: The single lien token is deposited into a bare EVK escrow vault as the line's only collateral, and the fixed 1e18 supply is what lets close reclaim exactly one token before burning.

docLink.label: Read more about Isolated Credit Lines

nodes.title: Oracle Registry
nodes.summary: The price source for every credit line's collateral: one cached USD mark per lien — determined by notary, or repurchase agreement.

eulerPrimitive.how: It is an Euler IPriceOracle adapter (quote-based _getQuote, PriceOracle_* errors, ScaleUtils math) that each line's frozen EulerRouter resolves the (lienToken, USDC) pair through.

docLink.label: Read more about Isolated Credit Lines

nodes.title: Credit Warehouse
nodes.summary:
The Credit Warehouse is a Gnosis Safe, which holds Euler Earn shares. Zodiac Roles protects operations on the Safe.

All USDC is either managed by Euler Earn, or in transit to the redemption queue.

eulerPrimitive.how: Its SUPPLY and REDEEM ops deposit into and redeem from the silo's EulerEarn USDC vault, always with the warehouse Safe as receiver and share owner (scope-pinned, never caller-chosen).

docLink.label: Read more about the Credit Warehouse

nodes.title: zipUSD Mint | Redeem
nodes.summary: zipUSD is a non-yield-bearing, synthetic credit-dollar, built on top of Euler Vault Kit.

eulerPrimitive.how: zipUSD is an Euler ESynth minted through the EVK capacity gate; supplied USDC parks in an Euler Earn pool.

nodes.title: ZipRedemptionQueue
nodes.summary: The senior exit: it redeems zipUSD for USDC at a strict one dollar per token, burning the zipUSD as USDC is delivered. It is treasury-internal plumbing, not an open market — exactly one requester (the redemption vault) is ever open, and it pays par regardless of impairment because the junior side absorbs losses. Par credits round down, so cumulative USDC out can never exceed cumulative USDC in; there is no pause, no upgrade, and no sweep, and the only USDC-out path is a claimant's own withdrawal.

eulerPrimitive.how: On settle it burns its own escrowed zipUSD through the ESynth burn seam (burn from own balance needs no allowance or minter capacity), dropping supply by exactly the filled amount.

nodes.title: Federation
nodes.summary: The machinery that lets many credit pools run under one shared senior dollar. A silo is one complete credit stack — venue adapter, warehouse Safe, EulerEarn pool, junior tranche — and each holds at most 28 lines, so scaling means more silos, all tied back to one mutualized zipUSD. The registry admits a silo only after a six-clause topology check proves its parts all point at their own pool, Safe, and oracle (never a neighbor's), counts open lines, and names where new loans route. The aggregator sums every silo's senior par-backing into one solvency number, reading the warehouse-owned position through ERC-4626 views so a token donation can never move the figure. Both are venue-agnostic: they ask each adapter for its senior pool through a venue-neutral getter.

members.name: SiloRegistry
members.blurb: The Timelock-owned catalog and admission gate: rejects mis-wired silos, caps each at 28 lines, and routes new loans to the current silo.

members.name: SeniorNavAggregator
members.blurb: Pure-view solvency telemetry: donation-immune sum of all silos' senior backing, compared against zipUSD supply (returns maximum coverage when supply is zero so a breaker cannot false-trip).

eulerPrimitive.how: Each silo's senior pool is a EulerEarn vault — the 28-line cap derives from its 30-market queue, admission proves adapter.seniorPool() == eePool, and backing is read through its donation-immune share-value views.

nodes.title: Loss engine
nodes.summary: The first-loss and insurance side. Every credit line is backed by a slashable xALPHA bond, and on a default two things happen: junior NAV is marked down by the recognized loss, and the bond is slashed as partial insurance. The coordinator is the only contract that can write the NAV impairment provision, bounded on-chain — down by atRisk times (1 minus recoveryFloor), healed up only by realized recovery receipts. The escrow custodies each line's bond with recorded amounts (donation-immune) and can only ever send xALPHA to three fixed destinations — the bond originator, the treasury Safe, or the junior tranche Safe — never an arbitrary address. Chainlink CRE drives both; a compromised CRE can grief but cannot steal.

members.name: DefaultCoordinator
members.blurb: The CRE-gated loss orchestrator: runs the lien status machine (Bonded to Defaulted to Resolved/WrittenOff), bounds the provision, and is the sole writeProvision caller into the NAV oracle.

members.name: LienXAlphaEscrow
members.blurb: Per-line xALPHA bond custody: locks at origination, returns on clean repayment, and on default slashes part to the treasury Safe and routes the remainder to the junior tranche Safe as the cohort premium.

nodes.title: Exit Gate
nodes.summary:
Zodiac Vault is an equity-based credit fund, built using the economic accounting engine of Moloch v3.

The LootERC20.sol contract represents a pro-rata share of the underlying NAV of the Zodiac Vault. However, if that capital is deployed in a line of credit, or earning yield in a Hydrex Pool — it is not available for Rage Quit.

If a Rage Quit is executed, when Zodiac Vault assets are deployed elsewhere — the equity simply burns, and returns back whatever liquid share happens to be in the vault at that time.

To prevent this error, all Loot Burns are held by the Exit Gate, and purchased through the CoW Orderbook at exit.

The Zodiac Vault itself purchases these shares through zipUSD redemption, and then burns the Loot.

nodes.title: NAV Oracle
nodes.summary:
NAV is the total value within the Zodiac Vault, as computed by SzipNavOracle. szipUSD yield is priced in NAV-per-share.

The NAV Oracle makes sure that each dollar of Credit Warehouse utilization is backed by the Zodiac Vault.

nodes.title: The auto-compounder engine
nodes.summary: A fleet of Zodiac modules on the engine Safe, each triggered by an off-chain keeper or CRE report, that runs the junior vault's yield flywheel: claim oHYDX option rewards from the Hydrex gauge, pay the USDC strike to exercise them into liquid HYDX, sell the HYDX on Algebra, rebuild and re-stake the zipUSD/xALPHA ICHI LP, and recycle the realized free value into NAV — or divert it to top up a recognized senior loss. The strike money comes from a leverage loop that borrows the warehouse's resting USDC against the vault's own LP on an isolated Euler vault, bounded by an aggregate cap and a borrow guard. The freeze module fences committed junior equity above the live senior coverage floor, and the buy-and-burn module is the protocol's only exit valve. No module holds funds; every output is hard-pinned to the vault.

members.name: HarvestVoteModule
members.blurb: Claims the gauge's oHYDX rewards into the vault, permanently locks a slice into vote power, votes the gauges, and claims anti-dilution rebases.

members.name: ExerciseModule
members.blurb: Pays a capped USDC strike to exercise oHYDX options into liquid HYDX minted straight to the vault, aborting instead of overpaying on a price spike.

members.name: SellModule
members.blurb: Three hard-pinned Algebra swaps — sell HYDX for USDC, buy and sell xALPHA against zipUSD — with a slippage floor and a per-call HYDX size cap.

members.name: LpStrategyModule
members.blurb: Builds, stakes, and unstakes the vault's zipUSD/xALPHA ICHI LP, and dissolves only LP that is excess over the solvency coverage floor.

members.name: RecycleModule
members.blurb: The free-value ledger: recycles credited yield into NAV-lifting backed zipUSD, or diverts USDC to the senior pool to heal a recognized loss, capped by the provision.

members.name: FarmUtilityLoopModule
members.blurb: Borrows the warehouse's resting USDC against the vault's LP on an isolated Euler vault to finance option strikes, then repays and releases the collateral.

members.name: OffRampModule
members.blurb: Turns the basket's idle zipUSD into USDC at par by driving the senior redemption queue, with every queue argument pinned to the vault.

members.name: SzipBuyBurnModule
members.blurb: The only exit valve: a resting CoW buy order for szipUSD at or below NAV minus a set discount, whose fills the Exit Gate burns.

members.name: DurationFreezeModule
members.blurb: Fences committed junior equity in a non-withdrawable sidecar Safe and never releases a move that would drop coverage below the live senior-liability floor.

nodes.title: Zodiac Vault
nodes.summary:
The Zodiac Vault is szipUSD's yield-bearing, Junior Tranche, built on Moloch v3, Gnosis Safe Zodiac modules, and Chainlink CRE.

Shares have an economic equity, which tracks the NAV of the vault.

• When yield accrues, share value increases.
• Should losses occur, share value decreases.

The Zodiac Vault also holds all Duration & Illiquidity Risk.

nodes.title: Chainlink CRE
nodes.tagline: The off-chain driver and price pusher
nodes.summary: Chainlink CRE is the protocol's off-chain operator: its keepers and signed reports trigger every engine module, push the two NAV price legs (xALPHA/USD and HYDX/USD) and the cross-chain xALPHA rate, drive the warehouse's four operations and the loss coordinator, and call the Exit Gate's burnFor after a buy-and-burn fill. Every report path is forwarder-gated and fail-closed — the CloneReportReceiver base leaves an unconfigured clone inert until the Timelock wires a trusted sender. Separately, the CREGatingHook installed on each credit line's borrow vault allows a borrow only when the account has authorized the line's CRE borrow driver, with an anti-spoof guard on the appended borrower identity. CRE is trusted for magnitude and timing, but each contract bounds a bad report to grief; the residual risk is those griefs firing correlated under one compromised workflow.

links.label: System seam map (the CRE driver class)

nodes.title: szALPHA bridge
nodes.summary: szALPHA is a liquid-staking token on Bittensor EVM (chain 964) wrapping ALPHA staked with a validator; its exchange rate rises as staking rewards accrue. A Chainlink CCT lane bridges it to Base: tokens are locked (never burned) in a lock-release pool on 964 and a mirror is minted on Base, so total supply and the exchange rate stay truthful. That Base mirror is the xALPHA leg the junior basket holds, LPs against zipUSD, and posts as per-line first-loss bonds. Zipcode trusts the CCT lock/release topology for supply conservation and a CRE job to push the validator's exchange rate to the Base rate oracle — NAV prices the leg off that pushed rate, and issuance fails closed when it is stale or unseeded.

members.name: SzAlpha (964)
members.blurb: The LST wrapper on Bittensor: mints szALPHA shares against validator stake, with a redeem that is never pausable by design.

members.name: SzAlphaLockReleasePool (964)
members.blurb: The CCIP endpoint on Bittensor that locks szALPHA (rather than burning it) when it bridges to Base.

members.name: SzAlphaMirror (Base)
members.blurb: The bridged szALPHA that actually circulates on Base — the basket's xALPHA leg.

members.name: SzAlphaTokenPool (Base)
members.blurb: The CCIP endpoint on Base: mints mirror tokens on arrival and burns them on exit, balancing supply across chains.

members.name: SzAlphaRateOracle (Base)
members.blurb: The on-chain feed a CRE job pushes the validator's exchange rate into — the freshness gate NAV issuance depends on.

nodes.title: CoW Limit Orderbook
nodes.summary:
The CoW Limit Orderbook is the only exit path for zipUSD depositors.

As lines of credit are repaid, users with pending exits are given priority before new credit is issued.

The CoW Limit Orderbook also enables outside bids to make offers on Zodiac Vault shares.

docLink.label: Read more about the CoW Limit Orderbook

nodes.title: Algebra + ICHI
nodes.summary: Algebra Integral is the AMM Hydrex runs on Base: the SellModule swaps through its router (HYDX to USDC, zipUSD to and from xALPHA), and its pools and TWAP plugin feed the fair-LP and NAV pricing. ICHI is the automated liquidity manager that holds the junior's zipUSD/xALPHA LP — single-sided deposits that ICHI balances into the underlying Algebra position, with the LP receipt token doubling as Euler collateral for the strike-financing loop. Zipcode trusts Algebra for honest swap execution and pool state, and ICHI to manage the position; the NAV oracle defends against manipulation by reconstructing the LP's reserves through the fair-reserves math rather than trusting spot.

members.name: Algebra SwapRouter
members.blurb: The router the SellModule's three hard-pinned market swaps go through.

members.name: Algebra pool + TWAP plugin
members.blurb: The pool state and time-weighted prices the fair-LP oracle and NAV oracle read.

members.name: ICHI vault
members.blurb: The managed-liquidity vault holding the junior's zipUSD/xALPHA LP; it runs the underlying Algebra position.

nodes.title: Hydrex
nodes.summary: Hydrex is a ve(3,3) DEX on Base, which holds single-sided LP, owned by the Zodiac Vault.

members.name: Gauge
members.blurb: The Zodiac Vault holds veHYDX, and votes to direct oHYDX emissions toward the protocol.

members.name: oHYDX option token
members.blurb: The Hydrex incentives token.

members.name: Voting escrow + voter
members.blurb: The permalock vote-power position the harvest module governs on behalf of the Zodiac vault.

nodes.title: Baal (Moloch v3)
nodes.summary: Baal is the Moloch v3 DAO container beneath the junior vault: a Zodiac module on the main Safe whose soulbound Loot token is the underlying economic share of the tranche. Depositors never touch Loot — the Exit Gate, granted the manager shaman, is the sole Loot custodian and mints or burns it 1:1 against the transferable szipUSD receipt. Baal's native ragequit is deliberately not wired, because it would pay the wrong amount against the free basket; exits run instead through the CoW buy-and-burn, and a full unwind is the same mechanism scaled up. Zipcode trusts Baal for the Loot mint/burn mechanics and the avatar (main Safe) linkage, nothing more.

nodes.title: zipUSD
nodes.tagline: the senior dollar — an Euler ESynth
nodes.summary: The shared senior dollar is not a token we wrote: it is Euler's ESynth, deployed with the three-argument constructor (EVC, "Zipcode USD", "zipUSD"), 18 decimals. Only capacity-granted minters can create it — the deposit module mints one-for-one by value against USDC in — and the redemption queue burns it at par through the allowance-free own-balance burn seam. Every zipUSD in circulation is backed by USDC sitting in the credit warehouse's EulerEarn pool.

eulerPrimitive.how: It IS an EVK-family primitive — Euler's ESynth (euler-vault-kit/src/Synths/ESynth.sol), governed by mint capacities rather than a custom token contract.

nodes.title: An isolated credit line
nodes.tagline: one lien, one EVK vault pair
nodes.summary:
Each Credit line is an isolated, single-use vault, with its own:

• Collateral Vault
• USDC Supply Vault
• Router & Oracle
• CRE Gating Hook
• Borrower-of-Record

eulerPrimitive.how: Both vaults are EVK proxies from the GenericFactory — isolation is the vault kit's own guarantee, not custom code.

docLink.label: Read more about Isolated Credit Lines

nodes.title: Farm Vault
nodes.summary:
The Farm Vault is a credit line dedicated to supplying the USDC needed to exercising oHYDX tokens.

The Zodiac Vault module responsible for this strategy is the sole borrower for this vault.

docLink.label: Read more, in the "Why Euler?" Blog

nodes.title: CRE Strategist
nodes.summary: The Zodiac Vault runs an auto-exercise strategy on Hydrex, and compounds that yield into single-sided zipUSD LP.

flows.title: Senior supply
flows.blurb: A lender's USDC enters through the deposit module, which mints zipUSD one-for-one by value and parks the USDC in the senior pool with the warehouse Safe as the share holder; the warehouse custodies the EulerEarn shares backing the zipUSD float.

flows.title: Open and draw a credit line
flows.blurb: The controller — the venue layer's only caller — opens each isolated line through the Euler venue adapter: the line's 1/1 lien token is its collateral, priced by the oracle registry (appraised value minus debt), and the CRE gating hook installed on the line's borrow vault lets only the account that authorized the line's borrow driver draw. Repayment is never gated.

flows.title: Senior par redemption
flows.blurb: zipUSD comes back to USDC at strict par through the redemption queue: the warehouse redeems EulerEarn shares and repays USDC into the queue, which burns zipUSD as claims fill. The queue is treasury-internal plumbing — the engine's off-ramp module is its driver, turning the basket's idle zipUSD into USDC — and it pays par regardless of impairment, because the junior side absorbs losses.

flows.title: Junior entry
flows.blurb: The default experience is the zap: the deposit module takes USDC, mints zipUSD, and hands it straight to the Exit Gate, which prices the deposit at the NAV oracle's entry price (max of spot and time-average, rounded down) and mints szipUSD paired 1:1 with Baal Loot held only by the gate.

flows.title: The yield flywheel
flows.blurb: Keepers drive the engine's loop: claim oHYDX from the Hydrex gauge, exercise it into HYDX by paying USDC strikes financed by borrowing warehouse USDC against the vault's own LP, sell the HYDX on Algebra, rebuild and re-stake the zipUSD/xALPHA ICHI LP, and recycle the realized free value into backed zipUSD that lifts NAV per share for every holder.

flows.title: Junior exit (buy and burn)
flows.blurb: There is no in-kind exit: a szipUSD holder rests a sell order on CoW, where the engine's buy-and-burn module maintains the protocol's backstop bid at or below the NAV exit price (min of spot and time-average) minus a discount. Bought szipUSD is retired at the Exit Gate with its paired Loot, ticking NAV per share up for stayers.

flows.title: Loss waterfall
flows.blurb: When a credit line defaults, the CRE-gated DefaultCoordinator writes an impairment provision into the NAV oracle — marking junior NAV down so szipUSD holders absorb the loss first — and slashes the line's xALPHA first-loss bond, part to the treasury and the remainder to the junior Safe. The engine's recycle module can then divert free value into the senior pool to heal the hole, capped by the live provision, so the shared zipUSD only ever sees what remains after the junior is exhausted.

flows.title: The cross-chain xALPHA mark
flows.blurb: szALPHA bridges from Bittensor to Base over a Chainlink CCT lock/release lane, and a CRE job pushes the validator's exchange rate to the Base rate oracle. The NAV oracle prices the basket's xALPHA leg off that pushed rate: a stale rate blocks issuance at the Exit Gate but never blocks exit.
